title: Add useShallow to DesignCanvas store selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders
description: DesignCanvas.tsx subscribes to nodes, edges, onNodesChange, onEdgesChange from canvas-store without useShallow. During simulation, this causes re-renders on every tick (~60/sec). Add `import { useShallow } from 'zustand/react/shallow'` and wrap selectors: `const nodes = useCanvasStore(useShallow(s => s.nodes))`.
acceptanceCriteria: All array/object selectors in DesignCanvas use useShallow
React DevTools Profiler shows reduced re-renders during simulation
No functional regression

title: Add typed config interface for Compute components — replace Record<string, ...>
description: Components in Compute (web-server, app-server, serverless, worker) currently use `config: Record<string, number | string | boolean>` which is completely untyped. Create a typed interface ComputeConfig with specific fields: instances (number), cpuCores (number), memoryGb (number), autoScale (boolean). Use discriminated union keyed on componentType.
acceptanceCriteria: ComputeConfig interface defined with all component-specific fields
All Compute components in palette-items use the typed config
TypeScript catches invalid config values at compile time

title: Remove Record<string, unknown> from SystemDesignNodeData — use explicit fields only
description: SystemDesignNodeData at types.ts:42 extends Record<string, unknown>, which allows arbitrary property access and defeats TypeScript's type checking. Remove the `extends Record<string, unknown>` and ensure all known fields are explicitly declared. This will surface type errors where code accesses non-existent properties on node data.
acceptanceCriteria: SystemDesignNodeData no longer extends Record<string, unknown>
All existing property accesses compile without errors (add missing fields if needed)
TypeScript strict mode catches invalid property access on node data

title: Type SimulationCommand as discriminated union — replace string type and unknown payload
description: SimulationCommand at types.ts:127-132 uses `type: string` and `payload: unknown`, providing no type safety for command dispatch. Replace with a discriminated union: `{ type: 'play'; payload: { speed: number } } | { type: 'pause'; payload: undefined } | { type: 'injectChaos'; payload: { eventType: string; nodeIds: string[] } } | ...`. Enumerate all command types from simulation-orchestrator usage.
acceptanceCriteria: SimulationCommand is a discriminated union with specific type/payload pairs
All dispatch sites pass type-checked payloads
TypeScript errors on unknown command types

title: Type SimulationEvent as discriminated union — replace string type and unknown data
description: SimulationEvent at types.ts:143-150 uses `type: string` and `data: unknown`, providing no type safety for event handling. Replace with a discriminated union: `{ type: 'node-failure'; data: { nodeId: string; reason: string } } | { type: 'recovery'; data: { nodeId: string } } | { type: 'alert'; data: { level: string; message: string } } | ...`. Enumerate all event types from chaos-engine and simulation-orchestrator usage.
acceptanceCriteria: SimulationEvent is a discriminated union with specific type/data pairs
All event handlers use narrowed types
TypeScript errors on unknown event types

title: Narrow componentType from string to ComponentType union in SystemDesignNodeData
description: SystemDesignNodeData at types.ts:46 declares `componentType: string` which allows any string value. Create a `ComponentType` union type from all palette-items component types (e.g. 'web-server' | 'load-balancer' | 'database' | 'redis' | ...) and use it instead of `string`. This provides autocomplete, prevents typos, and enables exhaustive pattern matching.
acceptanceCriteria: ComponentType union type defined with all valid component types
SystemDesignNodeData.componentType uses ComponentType instead of string
All palette-items and node creation code passes valid ComponentType values

title: Deduplicate NodeShape — remove local definition from BaseNode.tsx, keep canonical in types.ts
description: NodeShape is defined both in types.ts:100-108 and locally in BaseNode.tsx:14 (as `export type NodeShape = ...`). The BaseNode.tsx copy creates a parallel type that can drift out of sync. Remove the local definition from BaseNode.tsx and import from types.ts. Update CATEGORY_SHAPE in BaseNode.tsx to use the imported type.
acceptanceCriteria: BaseNode.tsx no longer defines its own NodeShape type
BaseNode.tsx imports NodeShape from @/lib/types
CATEGORY_SHAPE record in BaseNode.tsx uses the imported type
No other files import NodeShape from BaseNode

title: Unify NodeMetrics and NodeMetricsSnapshot into single base interface
description: NodeMetrics (types.ts:153-166) and NodeMetricsSnapshot (sim-metrics-bus.ts:44) represent overlapping concepts with different field sets. NodeMetrics has id/throughput/latency/errorRate/utilization/queueDepth. NodeMetricsSnapshot has similar fields but may drift. Unify into a single base interface in types.ts and have NodeMetricsSnapshot extend it with any additional fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Single base metrics interface in types.ts
NodeMetricsSnapshot extends the base or is replaced entirely
All consumers import from the canonical location
No duplicate field definitions

title: Align NodeType (chaos-engine.ts:33-45) with ComponentType union
description: chaos-engine.ts defines its own `NodeType` union ('load-balancer' | 'api-server' | 'web-server' | 'worker' | 'database' | 'cache' | 'queue' | 'storage' | 'cdn' | 'dns' | 'gateway' | 'service') that partially overlaps with but diverges from the componentType values in SystemDesignNodeData. Align by deriving NodeType from ComponentType or creating a mapping function, so chaos events can target all valid component types.
acceptanceCriteria: NodeType in chaos-engine.ts is derived from or aligned with ComponentType
No chaos event targets an invalid component type
Mapping between ComponentType and chaos NodeType is explicit and type-safe

title: Fix SSR hydration risk — move localStorage check in ui-store to onRehydrateStorage callback
description: ui-store.ts:122-123 reads `localStorage.getItem('architex_onboarding_completed')` during store initialization with a `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard. This is fragile for SSR — the guard works but the value is baked into the initial state before hydration. Move this check into zustand/persist's `onRehydrateStorage` callback so it runs after hydration, preventing potential SSR/client mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: localStorage read for onboarding state moved to onRehydrateStorage callback
No typeof window check in initial state definition
SSR renders with safe default, client hydrates with localStorage value
Onboarding flow works correctly in both SSR and client-only modes

title: Fix stale closure pattern in simulation-store setPlaybackSpeed
description: simulation-store.ts:211-216 setPlaybackSpeed reads `orchestratorRef` and `status` via `get()` then calls `set({ playbackSpeed: speed })` before using the ref. While the current implementation reads first then writes (correct order), the pattern should use `set` with a callback to ensure atomicity: `set((s) => { if (s.status === 'running' && s.orchestratorRef) s.orchestratorRef.setSpeed(speed); return { playbackSpeed: speed }; })`. This prevents a race where another action could modify state between the get() and set() calls.
acceptanceCriteria: setPlaybackSpeed uses atomic set callback pattern
No stale closure possible between reading orchestratorRef and setting speed
Simulation speed changes work correctly during active simulation

title: Replace btoa obfuscation with proper encryption for API key in ai-store
description: ai-store.ts:54-58 uses `btoa(key.split('').reverse().join(''))` to 'obfuscate' the API key before persisting to localStorage. This is trivially reversible and provides zero security. Replace with Web Crypto API (SubtleCrypto) using AES-GCM with a device-derived key, or at minimum use a proper encoding that doesn't give false security confidence. If full encryption is out of scope, remove the obfuscation entirely and add a comment explaining the security model (localStorage is inherently insecure, key is protected by browser same-origin policy).
acceptanceCriteria: btoa/atob obfuscation removed from ai-store.ts
API key either properly encrypted with Web Crypto API or stored plainly with documented security rationale
Existing stored keys are migrated on first load
Key retrieval works correctly after the change

title: Make orchestratorRef in simulation-store non-persisted explicitly
description: simulation-store.ts stores `orchestratorRef: SimulationOrchestrator | null` which is a live class instance reference. The store does not use zustand/persist, but if persistence is ever added, this ref would break serialization. Add an explicit comment and, if the store ever gains persist middleware, add it to the `partialize` exclusion list. Additionally, consider using `skipHydration` or a separate non-persisted store slice for runtime-only state.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestratorRef is documented as non-serializable runtime-only state
If simulation-store ever uses persist, orchestratorRef is excluded via partialize
No runtime regression in simulation lifecycle

title: Add exhaustive switch helpers for NodeCategory (14 values)
description: NodeCategory in types.ts has 14 values ('compute' | 'load-balancing' | 'storage' | 'messaging' | 'networking' | 'processing' | 'client' | 'observability' | 'security' | 'services' | 'fintech' | 'data-engineering' | 'ai-llm' | 'db-internals'). Add an `assertNeverCategory(value: never): never` exhaustive check helper and a `NODE_CATEGORIES` const array. This ensures switch statements covering all categories get compile-time errors when new categories are added.
acceptanceCriteria: assertNeverCategory helper exported from types.ts
NODE_CATEGORIES const array with all 14 values exported
At least one existing switch statement updated to use the exhaustive helper
Adding a new category without updating switches causes a TypeScript error

EDGE_TYPES const array with all 9 values exported
At least one existing switch statement updated to use the exhaustive helper

title: Add exhaustive switch helpers for SimulationStatus (5 values)
description: SimulationStatus in simulation-store.ts has 5 values ('idle' | 'running' | 'paused' | 'completed' | 'error'). Add an `assertNeverStatus(value: never): never` exhaustive check helper and a `SIMULATION_STATUSES` const array. Move the type to types.ts for canonical access. This ensures switch statements covering all statuses get compile-time errors when new statuses are added.
acceptanceCriteria: assertNeverStatus helper exported from types.ts
SIMULATION_STATUSES const array with all 5 values exported
SimulationStatus type moved to types.ts (re-exported from simulation-store for backward compat)
At least one existing switch statement updated to use the exhaustive helper

title: Document data flow pipeline with types at each step — architecture doc
description: Create a typed data flow architecture document showing the complete pipeline: PaletteItem (drag) -> SystemDesignNodeData (canvas) -> NodeMetrics (simulation) -> SimulationSnapshot (history) -> ExportPayload (export). Document the type transformations at each step, where data is enriched or narrowed, and which store owns each stage. Include a Mermaid diagram showing the flow with type annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture doc covers all 5 pipeline stages with TypeScript interfaces
Mermaid diagram shows data flow with types at each boundary
Each store's ownership of state is documented
Type transformation functions between stages are identified

title: Rename ai-store localStorage key from 'architex-ai-settings' to 'architex-ai' — standardize key format
description: ai-store.ts:198 uses persist key 'architex-ai-settings' which has an inconsistent suffix compared to other stores (e.g. 'architex-ui', 'architex-canvas', 'architex-progress'). Rename to 'architex-ai' for consistency. Add a migration in onRehydrateStorage to read from the old key and delete it on first load.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist key changed from 'architex-ai-settings' to 'architex-ai'
Migration reads old key on first load and copies to new key
Old key is cleaned up after migration
Existing users don't lose their AI settings

title: Rename billing-store localStorage key from 'architex:billing-store' to 'architex-billing' — fix colon separator
description: billing-store.ts:114 uses persist key 'architex:billing-store' which uses a colon separator instead of hyphens. All other stores use 'architex-{name}' format. Rename to 'architex-billing' for consistency. Add a migration in onRehydrateStorage to read from the old key and delete it on first load.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist key changed from 'architex:billing-store' to 'architex-billing'
Migration reads old key on first load and copies to new key
Old key is cleaned up after migration
Existing users don't lose their billing data

title: Rename onboarding localStorage key from 'architex_onboarding_completed' to 'architex-onboarding-completed' — fix underscores
description: ui-store.ts:123 reads 'architex_onboarding_completed' and onboarding-overlay.tsx:166 writes 'architex_onboarding_completed'. Both use underscores instead of hyphens. Rename to 'architex-onboarding-completed' for consistency with all other keys. Update both the read and write sites, and add a one-time migration to copy the old key value.
acceptanceCriteria: Both read (ui-store.ts) and write (onboarding-overlay.tsx) use 'architex-onboarding-completed'
Migration reads old underscore key and writes to new hyphen key on first load
Old key is cleaned up after migration
Existing users who completed onboarding don't see it again

title: Rename consent localStorage key from 'architex_consent' to 'architex-consent' — fix underscores
description: consent.ts:21 exports CONSENT_STORAGE_KEY = 'architex_consent' which uses underscores instead of hyphens. Rename to 'architex-consent' for consistency with the standard key format. Update the constant and add a one-time migration to copy the old key value.
acceptanceCriteria: CONSENT_STORAGE_KEY changed from 'architex_consent' to 'architex-consent'
Migration reads old underscore key and writes to new hyphen key on first load
Old key is cleaned up after migration
Existing users' consent preferences are preserved

title: Rename mobile advisory localStorage key from 'architex_mobile_advisory_dismissed' to 'architex-mobile-advisory-dismissed' — fix underscores
description: MobileAdvisory.tsx:9 uses STORAGE_KEY = 'architex_mobile_advisory_dismissed' which uses underscores instead of hyphens. Rename to 'architex-mobile-advisory-dismissed' for consistency with the standard key format. Add a one-time migration to copy the old key value.
acceptanceCriteria: STORAGE_KEY changed from 'architex_mobile_advisory_dismissed' to 'architex-mobile-advisory-dismissed'
Migration reads old underscore key and writes to new hyphen key on first load
Old key is cleaned up after migration

title: Standardize billing usage-tracker localStorage keys from colon to hyphen separator
description: usage-tracker.ts:13-14 uses 'architex:billing:usage' and 'architex:billing:period-start' with colon separators. Rename to 'architex-billing-usage' and 'architex-billing-period-start' for consistency with the standard hyphen-separated key format. Add one-time migration to copy old key values.
acceptanceCriteria: STORAGE_KEY changed from 'architex:billing:usage' to 'architex-billing-usage'
PERIOD_KEY changed from 'architex:billing:period-start' to 'architex-billing-period-start'
Migration reads old colon keys and writes to new hyphen keys on first load

title: Standardize collaboration localStorage keys from colon to hyphen separator
description: comments.ts:26 uses 'architex:comments' and upvotes.ts:10-11 uses 'architex:upvotes' / 'architex:user-upvotes' with colon separators. Rename to 'architex-comments', 'architex-upvotes', 'architex-user-upvotes' for consistency with the standard hyphen-separated key format. Add one-time migration to copy old key values.
acceptanceCriteria: All three keys renamed from colon to hyphen format
Migration reads old colon keys and writes to new hyphen keys on first load

title: Standardize sync-bridge localStorage key prefix from colon to hyphen separator
description: sync-bridge.ts:42 uses STORAGE_PREFIX = 'architex:diagram:' with colon separators. Rename to 'architex-diagram-' for consistency with the standard hyphen-separated key format. Add a migration that scans localStorage for keys matching the old prefix pattern and renames them.
acceptanceCriteria: STORAGE_PREFIX changed from 'architex:diagram:' to 'architex-diagram-'
Migration scans for old-prefix keys and renames to new prefix
Old keys are cleaned up after migration
Existing saved diagrams are accessible under the new key format
